speaker: Dr. Amelia Chen
title: Gestural Latency in Wearable Controllers: A Field Study
abstract: We report results from a six-month field deployment of three wearable gestural controllers with *forty non-expert users*. End-to-end latency above 35 ms correlated with measurable drops in self-reported expressivity — but only for dynamic articulations. Static gestures tolerated far more latency than the literature predicts.

speaker: David Park
title: Haptic Feedback Grammars for Accessibility Interfaces
abstract: A small grammar of haptic primitives — *tap*, *brush*, *pulse*, *drag* — is sufficient to encode the majority of micro-interactions in three widely-used accessibility toolkits. We release an open-source library implementing the grammar and evaluate it with eight screen-reader users.

speaker: Dr. Mariana Souza
title: Embodied Metaphors in Everyday Interaction Design
abstract: We trace the migration of embodied metaphors from HCI research into mass-market consumer devices over the past fifteen years. **Three patterns dominate**: container, path, and force. We argue the remaining design space is substantially larger than the literature currently explores.

speaker: Dr. Marta Li
title: Foundations of Creative Computing
abstract: Dr. Li's keynote traces a line from *early algorithmic art* — Nake, Molnár, Cohen — through contemporary generative systems, asking what changes when the materials of artistic practice become themselves programmable.

speaker: Prof. Rohan Patel
title: Algorithmic Ecologies
abstract: **Algorithmic ecologies** frames generative systems as embedded in cultural, material, and political contexts rather than as autonomous technical objects. Prof. Patel argues that the next decade of creative computing research needs to take these contexts as first-class subject matter.

speaker: Elane Rodriguez
title: Designing Tangible Controllers
abstract: A hands-on workshop covering the full design cycle for a tangible controller: material sketches, microcontroller integration, firmware, and host-side mapping. Participants leave with a working prototype.

speaker: Alexander Ivanov
title: Browser-based Creative Coding
abstract: Modern browsers ship with WebAudio, WebMIDI, WebGL, and WebGPU. This workshop is a tour through the stack with small exercises at each layer. No prior JavaScript experience is required.

speaker: Priya Shah
title: Generative Audio for Live Performance
abstract: A practical workshop on integrating small generative audio models into live performance rigs. We cover latency management, prompt design for audio models, and hybrid hand/AI control mapping.

speaker: Lina Okafor
title: Threshold: Ambient Field Recordings
abstract: *Threshold* is an installation of sixteen speakers distributed along a corridor. As visitors pass through the space they trigger slow crossfades between field recordings made at the boundary between urban and rural environments across three continents.

speaker: Kenji Maeda
title: Feedback Garden
abstract: *Feedback Garden* runs as a standing installation during the exhibition and is activated as a scored performance during the evening. Each iteration produces a different state in the system, so the performance version is never identical to the walk-through version.

speaker: Priya Shah
title: Resonant Bodies
abstract: A duet for two performers wearing contact-microphone vests. The vests' output is processed through a generative feedback network that learns from the previous minute of performance and shapes the subsequent minute accordingly.

speaker: Samuel Ortiz
title: Drift States
abstract: A solo performance exploring long-form drift, with slowly evolving textures driven by a custom granular synthesizer. *Drift States* has been performed in four cities over the past year; each performance is unique.

speaker: Jun Watanabe
title: Latency Compensation for Collaborative Browser Instruments
abstract: A lightweight protocol for compensating round-trip latency in browser-based collaborative instruments. We show playable ensembles can be sustained across intercontinental links when *asymmetric* compensation is used.

speaker: Aisha Bello
title: Teaching Live Coding Remotely: Lessons from Three Terms
abstract: Reflections on three terms of teaching live coding as a fully remote cohort. **Key findings**: latency shapes pedagogy as much as content choice; asynchronous listening parties were more effective than synchronous jams.

speaker: Noam Eliyahu
title: A Mobile Toolkit for Sensor Sonification
abstract: An open-source toolkit that turns smartphone sensors into a live sonification stream over WebRTC. We demonstrate three use cases: fitness feedback, architectural acoustics, and sensor-driven ensemble performance.
